notes.anchor: to bring the legions across from Africa and from Sardinia
notes.note: Decimus Brutus presses the Senate to ferry the garrison legions stationed in the provinces of Africa and Sardinia over to Italy to reinforce the war effort after the relief of Mutina.

notes.anchor: whether or not to send for Brutus
notes.note: The Brutus to be "sent for" is Marcus Brutus the Liberator, then commanding in Macedonia and the East; bringing his army to Italy was repeatedly urged in this correspondence.

notes.anchor: whether they will grant me pay or only decree it
notes.note: Decimus distinguishes a senatorial decree (decernere) authorizing funds from the actual disbursement of pay (stipendium) for his troops, a recurring grievance as the relieving armies went unrewarded.

The dateline "iii Non. Iun." (the third day before the Nones of June, counted inclusively, the Nones falling on the 5th) is 3 June 43 BC; Decimus wrote on the march toward Cularo (Grenoble) in pursuit of Antony.
